title: Te Whakamahi i te Mātauranga Taketake ki te Hoahoa Whare: Applying Indigenous Knowledge to Sustainable Building Design
subject: Technology
culturalContext: Students will explore how Ngāti Kahungunu building traditions and environmental knowledge from the Mangakōtukutuku Stream catchment can inform modern sustainable architecture. The lesson connects to local pūrākau about Rongokako and the traditional pā sites along the stream, examining how tūpuna used natural materials, passive heating/cooling, and watershed management principles that remain relevant today.
learningObjectives: Analyze traditional Ngāti Kahungunu building techniques to identify at least three sustainable design principles applicable to modern construction
Design a scale model of a sustainable building that incorporates both mātauranga Māori and contemporary technology solutions
Evaluate the environmental impact of building materials using both scientific methods and cultural sustainability frameworks
Present design solutions that demonstrate understanding of kaitiakitanga responsibilities specific to the Mangakōtukutuku catchment area

activities.title: Whakapapa o te Whare: Tracing Building Traditions
activities.description: Visit local marae and historical sites to document traditional building methods. Students create digital portfolios comparing Ngāti Kahungunu architectural features with modern sustainable building standards.

activities.title: Te Awa hei Kaiwhakaako: The Stream as Teacher
activities.description: Conduct water quality testing along Mangakōtukutuku Stream while learning from kaumātua about traditional environmental indicators. Students correlate scientific data with mātauranga about watershed health.

activities.title: Mahi Rauora: Materials Science Workshop
activities.description: Test traditional building materials (harakeke, raupō, tōtara) alongside modern alternatives using technology equipment to compare thermal properties, durability, and environmental impact.

activities.title: Hoahoa Auaha: Integrated Design Challenge
activities.description: Design and construct scale models of sustainable buildings that must meet both modern building code requirements and cultural design principles from Ngāti Kahungunu traditions.

activities.title: Whakawhiti Kōrero: Community Presentation
activities.description: Present design solutions to local kaumātua, builders, and environmental planners, explaining how their designs honor cultural knowledge while addressing contemporary sustainability challenges.

resources: Ngāti Kahungunu kaumātua and local builders knowledgeable in traditional construction
Mangakōtukutuku Stream water testing kits and environmental monitoring equipment
'Te Ara Taketake: Indigenous Building Traditions of Hawke's Bay' digital archive
3D modeling software and material testing equipment from college technology department

tasks: Digital portfolio documenting traditional building analysis
Scale model of sustainable building with integrated cultural features
Written report justifying material choices using both scientific and cultural frameworks

nzcAlignment: Technological Practice: Planning for practice, brief development, outcome development and evaluation
Technological Knowledge: Understanding technological modelling and products
Nature of Technology: Characteristics of technology and characteristics of technological outcomes
Key Competencies: Participating and contributing, relating to others
